revision.text:
[[de:Abesches]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |TITEL = Abesches |NAME3 = D36-D58 N37-S29-I12 |NAME3-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME3-ERKLÄRUNG = Abesches
''[[File:U-A725 LATIN SMALL LETTER EGYPTOLOGICAL AIN.gif|7px|Egyptological Ajin]]bšs''
''The Encompassing (Serpent)'' }} '''Abesches''' (also spelled '''Sebeches''') was the {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|qid=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egyptian|1=Ancient Egypt}} name for a {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|qid=Q8928|lt=constellation|1=Constellation}} frequently recorded on the {{ill|diagonal star clocks|de|Diagonalsternuhr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clocks|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diagonal star clocks|qid=Q142353|1=Star clock}} of the {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}}. Abesches included the {{ill|Decanal stars|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/|lt=Decanal stars|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/|lt=Decanal stars|qid=Q804527}} of the {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|qid=Q5248738|lt=Decan (Egyptian astronomy)|1=Decan}} known as '''Sebeschsen'''. == Background == The constellation Abesches was composed of parts of the modern constellations {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|en|Virgo (constellation)|qid=Q8842|lt=Virgo (constellation)|1=Virgo (constellation)}} ('''Spica''') and {{ill|Centaurus|en|Centaurus|qid=Q8844|lt=Centaurus|1=Centaurus}} ('''Menkent'''). It was associated with the god {{ill|Min (god)|en|Min (god)|qid=Q192322|lt=Min (Egyptian mythology)|1=Min (god)}} and referenced the goddess {{ill|Shesmetet|en|Shesmetet|qid=Q19285|lt=Schesemtet|1=Shesmetet}}, the '''mother of the dead and kings''' and the '''destroyer of Osiris' enemies'''. Iconographically, the deity representing the constellation was depicted in human form, sometimes with a {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=falcon-headed|1=Horus}} appearance ('''Horus''') or a {{ill|Sekhmet|en|Sekhmet|qid=Q146104|lt=lion-headed|1=Sekhmet}} form ('''Sekhmet'''). On its head, it bore the {{ill|Uraeus|en|Uraeus|qid=Q756990|lt=Uraeus|1=Uraeus}}, flanked by two raised '''flails''' ('''{{ill|flagellum (Egyptian mythology)|de|Flagellum (ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flagellum (Egyptian mythology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flagellum (Egyptian mythology)|qid=Q3347009|1=Nekhakha flail}}'''). During the annual '''{{ill|Min festival|en|Min festival|qid=Q1936130|lt=Min festival|1=Min festival}} processions''', the '''constellation of Min''' was celebrated in connection with its decanal star '''Spica'''.{{ill|Catherine Graindorge|de|Catherine Graindorge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catherine Graindorge|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catherine Graindorge|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}: ''Vom weißen Stier des Min zu Amenemope: Metamorphosen eines Ritus'' In: Carola Metzner-Nebelsick: ''Rituale in der Vorgeschichte, Antike und Gegenwart – Studien zur Vorderasiatischen, Prähistorischen und Klassischen Archäologie, Ägyptologie, Alten Geschichte, Theologie und Religionswissenschaft; Interdisziplinäre Tagung vom 1.–2. Februar 2002 an der Freien Universität Berlin.'' Leidorf, Rahden 2003, ISBN 3-89646-434-5, pp. 37–43. In the '''decan lists''' of the '''{{ill|Book of Nut|en|Book of Nut|qid=Q2005559|lt=Nutbook|1=Book of Nut}} (Sethos text)''', the decan '''Sebeschsen''' represented the '''eighth decan''' along the '''body of the goddess {{ill|Nut (goddess)|en|Nut (goddess)|qid=Q165496|lt=Nut (Egyptian mythology)|1=Nut (goddess)}}'''. It served as a dating reference based on its arrangement under '''{{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|qid=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} (12th Dynasty)''' in '''his seventh regnal year'''. == References == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qid=Q979923|1=Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qid=Q2643704|1=Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. == Notes == {{Navigationsleiste Ägyptische Astronomie|qq=}} {{translated page|de|Abesches|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Abesches]] {{wikidata link|Q319775}}
